exemplar.title: How has Twitter rebranding to X affected user engagement and brand perception?

detailedExemplarData.title: How has Twitter rebranding to X affected user engagement and brand perception?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

files.fileName: Krishna MAHTANI [12H2] - KRISHNA MAHTANI 2025 Business Management IA  (1).pdf

strengths: Clearly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept and repeatedly ties discussion back to this concept
Consistently connects examples and data to Twitter/X, maintaining a coherent organisational focus
weaknesses: Analysis treats the concept as a label rather than integrating change-management theories (e.g. Lewin, Kotter)
Theoretical dimensions of âchangeâ are rarely invoked to deepen discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place understanding and organisational connection in bands 3â4. The student shows strong grasp of change and links sections to Twitter/X, justifying a 4, but lacks the fully integrated theoretical analysis needed for the top mark.

strengths: Documents are current, relevant to user engagement and brand metrics
Sources provide sufficient depth to support key points in the analysis
weaknesses: Exceeds the optimal number of documents, diluting focus
Some sources (e.g. LinkedIn post, blog) are superficial and lack rigorous data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Quantity and relevance align with bands 3â4, but overabundance of sources and variable depth best fit the 3-mark level rather than the highest band.

strengths: Appropriate selection of SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff Matrix tools
Applied each tool to Twitter/X with illustrative statistics
weaknesses: Application remains descriptive and lacks explicit causal linkage to the research question
Choice of tools is not justified through theoretical rationale or cross-tool synthesis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand feedback rates both selection and application at band 3. Tools are relevant and adequately used, but the analysis is not sufficiently integrated or critically justified for higher marks.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Effectively uses data (e.g. 13% user loss, 63% increase in likes) to support key points
Identifies and evaluates ethical improvements and engagement outcomes
weaknesses: Analysis is uneven, with limited triangulation or critical comparison of datasets
Integration across sections and deeper evaluation of implications is brief
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection and analytical quality align with the âsome analysisâ descriptor in band 3. The work demonstrates adequate selection and evaluation but does not reach the sustained integration of higher bands.

strengths: Conclusions restate findings consistently with parts of the evidence
Explicitly answers the research question regarding the effectiveness of the rebranding
weaknesses: Claims (e.g. âlargely effectiveâ) overgeneralise beyond mixed evidence
Concluding statements lack qualification and explicit linkage to specific data points
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place consistency and answer quality at band 2. The conclusion addresses the question but lacks the thorough alignment with evidence needed for the top band.

strengths: Logical structure with clear headings and sub-headings throughout
Comprehensive Table of Contents mirrors the reportâs flow
weaknesses: Structure is sound but offers no innovative organisational features
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The reportâs organisation fully meets the criteria for the highest band (2), showing an appropriate structure throughout.

strengths: Includes all required IB elements: title page with word count, candidate declaration, bibliography, appendices
Presentation is clean with section titles and consistent formatting
weaknesses: Bibliographic entries use inconsistent citation styles
Minor redundancy in headers (e.g. repeated title)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation elements are present and correct, placing the work in the top band for presentation (2), despite minor formatting inconsistencies.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical ToC with labelled sections (Introduction, Main Body, Conclusion, Works Cited).  Headings and sub-headings are consistent, so an âappropriate structure throughoutâ is demonstrated.

The chosen articles and reports are current and directly relate to user numbers, brand index scores, and perceptions; hence relevance is sound.  Depth is variable: many figures are single data points without further breakdown, and some sources (LinkedIn post, blog) are superficial.  Overall they are ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than âin-depth and providing a range of ideas,â fitting the 3-mark descriptor.
All required IB elementsâtitle page with word count and candidate declaration, section titles, bibliography, and appendicesâare present.  Therefore the highest presentation band is achieved.

At least six distinct contemporary documents are cited (BBC, YouGov, Verge, Business Insider, CMSWire, Anchor Digital).  Even if some are combined in appendices, the total clearly falls within the 3-5 range and meets the upper band for quantity.

Each tool is applied to Twitter/X with illustrative statistics (-13 % daily users, +63 % likes, 38 % new sign-ups aged 18-24).  However, many points remain descriptive and do not explicitly link back to the research question; underlying causal logic and cross-tool synthesis are limited.  Relevance is clear but application is only âadequatelyâ effective, meriting 3 marks.

The student selects data sets on usage, engagement metrics and demographics, but coverage is uneven and sometimes anecdotal.  Data are inserted into each analytical section but not triangulated or critically compared.  This corresponds to âadequate selection and use.â

Analysis does more than describe: it identifies strengths/weaknesses, opportunities and links them to engagement/perception outcomes.  Still, integration among tools is limited and evaluation of implications is brief.  The work fits the descriptor of âsome analysis of the research question,â justifying 3 marks.

The research question is explicitly answered (âthe changes have been largely effectiveâ), but explanation of the extent and any qualification is thin.  Hence this is more than a superficial nod yet falls short of a fully justified answer, matching the 2-mark descriptor.

SWOT, STEEPLE and Ansoff Matrix are appropriate strategic tools for assessing re-branding effects, giving an âadequate selection.â  The investigation omits other highly relevant frameworks (e.g. brand equity models, KPI trend charts), so effectiveness is moderate rather than wide-ranging.

The conclusion restates key findings and generally aligns with cited statistics, but several assertions (e.g. âlargely effectiveâ re-branding) are broader than the evidence presented.  Consistency exists in parts yet is not thorough, aligning with the 2-mark level.

All sections are explicitly tied to Twitter/X.  Statistics on daily users, index scores, sign-up demographics, and references to internal analytics show that the organisation is consistently in focus.  Nevertheless, the link is sometimes descriptive rather than woven into an integrated argument (e.g. STEEPLE lists factors without analysing how each specifically alters engagement).  This aligns with âstrong analysis with partial integration,â warranting 4 marks.

The student clearly identifies âchangeâ as the key concept and briefly explains why the re-branding is an example of change.  References to âchangeâ re-appear in the introduction, throughout the SWOT paragraph ("strengths of this change"), in STEEPLE ("market characterised by change"), and in the conclusion ("critical role of change as a dynamic concept").  However, the concept is treated mostly as a label; the analysis rarely uses the theoretical dimensions of change management (e.g. drivers, resistance, Lewin, Kotter) to deepen discussion.  Thus understanding is sound but not deeply integrated throughout, matching the descriptor for a 3-mark band.

annotations.comment: The strengths paragraph outlines reputational benefits but lacks quantitative support from the datasets. Incorporate specific metrics (e.g. sentiment scores) to substantiate these claims.
Citing the 13% user loss anchors the weakness insight. This effective use of data strengthens the analysis of change impact.
The opportunity section mentions an index score of â1.5 but does not explain how this translates into positive change. Could you clarify why a negative index indicates improvement?
The research question is clearly articulated at the outset, which provides a strong focus for the investigation and guides the structure of the report.

Labeling âChangeâ as the key concept is a starting point, but the analysis rarely invokes theoretical dimensions (e.g. Lewinâs model or Kotterâs steps) to deepen insights. Consider integrating these frameworks.
The Table of Contents is comprehensive and mirrors the structure of the report, aiding navigation and reinforcing a logical flow.
The introduction provides useful background on the rebranding but could benefit from explicitly linking each contextual detail back to the concept of change to strengthen theoretical integration.
Defining user engagement and linking it to the research question demonstrates clarity of terms, which strengthens the foundation for subsequent analysis.
The explanation of SWOT is descriptive but lacks reference to why this tool is particularly apt for analysing a change-management scenario. Consider justifying the choice with theory.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The environmental factor is briefly noted but lacks connection to the key concept of sustainability or data on user attitudes toward eco-friendly digital practices.
Highlighting updated moderation policies and user feedback mechanisms provides strong evidence of ethical change initiatives.
The legal section describes trademark steps but could reference challenges or delays encountered, which would enrich evaluation of change-related risks.
The acknowledgment of STEEPLE limitations is insightful. You could deepen this critique by suggesting alternative or complementary tools (e.g. PESTEL cross-impact analysis).
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The threats paragraph articulates competitive risks well but would be stronger if linked to brand-perception survey data or user retention figures.
The social factor discussion identifies community identity shifts, but lacks evidence of actual user behaviour change (e.g. engagement rates). Consider adding comparative statistics.
Good attention to the technological investments needed for a âsuper app.â Integrate an innovation-adoption theory to link this to the change management concept.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The economic analysis explains ad balance well but could be deepened by critiquing potential revenue loss scenarios if users disengage due to ad overload.
Acknowledging ethical improvements such as transparency reports strengthens the discussion of stakeholder trust impacts.
Reporting a 63% increase in likes aptly demonstrates market penetration effects. The data use here effectively supports the strategic insight.
Bibliographic entries vary in format and punctuation, which undermines presentation consistency. Adopt a single citation style (e.g. APA or MLA) across all sources.
The number of supporting documents exceeds five, which falls outside the ideal range. Consider consolidating or prioritising the most impactful sources.
The repeated title on the final page replicates the header from page 1. Eliminating duplication will improve overall presentation and reduce redundancy.
Citing 38% of new sign-ups from 18â24-year-olds enriches the argument for market development and ties back to engagement metrics.
The product development section effectively uses the 20% adoption statistic to illustrate feature uptake, reinforcing the link to user engagement.
Discussing the 10% trial of X Business clearly supports the diversification argument and demonstrates effective use of internal analytics.
The conclusion claims the rebranding has been âlargely effective,â but this overgeneralizes beyond the mixed evidence. Temper statements to align with the data presented.
Refine the concluding statements by explicitly linking each claim to specific evidence (e.g. engagement metrics or brand-index changes) to enhance consistency.
